hypothesis: If the Force Delta (U∆)—the RMS difference between conservative forces (−∇Ê) and directly predicted non‑conservative forces (F̂nc) from a single NNIP—is a valid uncertainty metric, then U∆ will positively correlate with the true force error (εdirect) on both equilibrium and adversarially perturbed out‑of‑distribution structures and will outperform intra‑family ensemble variance in identifying high‑error predictions.
